conceptClusters.title: Retrieved element and tree rules
exitEvidence: Learner retrieves complete element boundaries and parsed relationships before adding source-syntax complexity.
Learner uses allowed-content evidence instead of appearance when diagnosing a source-to-tree mismatch.

conceptClusters.title: Source tokens and parsed nodes
exitEvidence: Learner maps source tag ranges to resulting element and text nodes without inventing closing-tag nodes.
Learner explains a changed serialization-to-DOM difference using parser evidence rather than appearance.

conceptClusters.title: Void elements and attribute grammar
exitEvidence: Learner authors void elements without invented end tags or XHTML slash assumptions.
Learner places separated attribute names and quoted values inside start-tag boundaries and verifies parsed values.

conceptClusters.title: Attribute value contracts
exitEvidence: Learner predicts presence, absence, valid, invalid, and changed values according to each attribute contract.
Learner distinguishes boolean, enumerated, token-list, identifier, URL, and free-text behavior.

conceptClusters.title: Comments references and parser recovery
exitEvidence: Learner uses bounded author comments and character references without treating either as page structure or secret storage.
Learner predicts browser recovery, repairs the causal source defect, and verifies a stable changed tree.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A worked service note exposes how source tokens, attributes, void syntax, comments, references, and parser recovery become a stable DOM.
activityDesigns.startingState: A valid first-module notice and a synchronized source, token, DOM, preview, and parser view are open.
activityDesigns.completionState: The learner has predicted and explained twelve source-to-DOM contrasts while retaining element, tree, and content-model reasoning.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback contrasts predicted source tokens with observed parsed nodes, attributes, recovery, and changed-case behavior before naming the rule.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Worked guidance reveals one tokenizer or tree-construction observation at a time after the learner commits a prediction.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss returns to a smaller contrasting fragment, requires a revised source-to-DOM map, and then checks a changed fragment.

interactions.title: Retrieve complete element anatomy
interactions.learnerAction: Label the complete element, both tag boundaries, content, parent, and next sibling before the syntax model adds attributes.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; prior element and tree evidence is retrieved.

evidence.observable: Labels preserve complete boundaries and actual parsed relationships.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Prior element or tree concepts are missing before new syntax.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild one complete element and its sibling path.

interactions.title: Map tags to one element node
interactions.learnerAction: Map opening and closing source-tag ranges to the single parsed element node and its text child.
interactions.artifactChange: Token and DOM ranges are highlighted without source change.

evidence.observable: Both source tags map to one element while content maps to a text node.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A separate DOM node invented for the closing tag.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace one element from source ranges to parsed node.

interactions.title: Predict serialization differences
interactions.learnerAction: Predict which authored characters disappear, normalize, or remain when source is parsed and serialized from the DOM.
interactions.artifactChange: A comparison serialization is generated from the DOM.

evidence.observable: Prediction separates authored token spelling from stable node structure.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Assumption that DOM serialization must be byte-identical to source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare one source token, parsed property, and serialized result.

interactions.title: Explain tag node and element terms
interactions.learnerAction: Explain tag, element, element node, and text node using one selected source-to-DOM path.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; a terminology explanation is recorded.

evidence.observable: Each term names a distinct source range or parsed object in the worked note.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Definitions repeated without mapping to observed evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Point to one concrete source range or node per term.

interactions.title: Predict void-element parsing
interactions.learnerAction: Predict nodes and following text when a void element is authored without an end tag in the worked note.
interactions.artifactChange: A void element is inserted before ordinary following text.

evidence.observable: Prediction creates one void element node and keeps following text outside it.
evidence.changedCase: A different void element receives attributes and new following text.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Following text predicted as void-element content.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare normal-element and void-element content capability.

interactions.title: Challenge the trailing slash myth
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect how an HTML trailing slash affects neither void closure nor a normal element's need for an end tag.
interactions.artifactChange: Slash and no-slash comparisons are parsed side by side.

evidence.observable: Observed nodes reject XHTML self-closing assumptions in HTML.
evidence.changedCase: A normal element receives a slash and following content is inspected.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Slash treated as a universal self-closing operator.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect the same slash on one void and one normal element.

interactions.title: Locate attribute grammar
interactions.learnerAction: Locate start-tag boundary, attribute name, separator, quotes, value, and whitespace in a valid unfamiliar attribute.
interactions.artifactChange: A valid start tag receives syntax annotations.

evidence.observable: All attribute parts stay inside the opening tag and values retain exact boundaries.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute placed after the tag or inside the closing tag.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reconstruct one opening tag from labeled grammar parts.

interactions.title: Predict parsed attribute values
interactions.learnerAction: Predict parsed names and values for quoted, unquoted, missing-separator, and duplicate-name source cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Four disposable start tags are parsed for comparison.

evidence.observable: Prediction distinguishes valid grammar, ambiguity, parse errors, and recovery.
evidence.changedCase: A fifth case changes whitespace and quote style.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute behavior predicted from appearance or string presence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tokenize one name-separator-value sequence at a time.

interactions.title: Contrast boolean and enumerated values
interactions.learnerAction: Predict behavior for absent, present, false-spelled boolean, valid enumerated, and invalid enumerated values.
interactions.artifactChange: Five attribute-state examples are evaluated without saving.

evidence.observable: Prediction uses presence for boolean state and defined keywords for enumerated state.
evidence.changedCase: A parallel boolean and enumerated pair changes presence and keyword.

interactions.feedbackTarget: String truthiness applied to all attribute value types.
interactions.correctionRoute: State each attribute's contract before reading its text.

interactions.title: Classify attribute value contracts
interactions.learnerAction: Classify token-list, identifier, URL, and free-text attributes and inspect how changed values parse.
interactions.artifactChange: Four attribute values are mutated in disposable examples.

evidence.observable: Each parsed value and behavior follows its element-specific contract.
evidence.changedCase: Whitespace, URL, identifier, and free-text values change independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All values treated as arbitrary interchangeable strings.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify the consuming attribute contract for one value.

interactions.title: Inspect comments and references
interactions.learnerAction: Locate a bounded author comment and a character reference in source, DOM, and visible output, then identify unsafe secret content.
interactions.artifactChange: A safe note and reserved text character join the model artifact.

evidence.observable: Comment remains inspectable source data while reference becomes intended text, not structure.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Comment assumed secret or character reference treated as an element.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect page source and DOM separately from visible output.

interactions.title: Explain parser recovery end to end
interactions.learnerAction: Predict, inspect, repair, and explain one malformed start tag whose recovery changes attributes and a following tree relationship.
interactions.artifactChange: The model artifact returns to a conforming stable parsed tree.

evidence.observable: Explanation links causal source defect, recovered nodes, minimal repair, and valid relationship.
evidence.changedCase: A different attribute defect and sibling insertion preserve the repair rule.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Recovery described as browser magic without causal source evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace tokenizer input, parsed property, tree effect, and repair.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A volunteer orientation page is annotated, extended with behavior-bearing attributes, and repaired through source-token, DOM, parser, and changed-case evidence.
activityDesigns.startingState: A valid heading, prose, and list artifact plus a volunteer brief are saved; source syntax beyond normal element boundaries is absent.
activityDesigns.completionState: The saved orientation page uses valid void syntax, attribute contracts, comments, references, and stable parser-clean relationships under mutations.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names the observed source token, parsed node or attribute, resulting behavior, unmet concept, and smallest next diagnostic action.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Hints progress from the intended source-to-DOM contract, to the relevant token boundary, to a smaller parallel fragment without final source.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Each failure requires a prediction, minimal repair, exact parsed evidence, and a changed volunteer-data case before the checkpoint passes.

interactions.title: Retrieve orientation structure
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the current heading, paragraph, list, parent-child, and sibling evidence before inspecting the saved orientation page.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; prior structural evidence is predicted.

evidence.observable: Prediction recovers complete boundaries and valid parsed relationships.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Missing prerequisite structure before syntax work.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reconstruct the smallest heading-paragraph-list subtree.

interactions.title: Map orientation source tokens
interactions.learnerAction: Map each heading tag, paragraph tag, list tag, and text range to the resulting DOM nodes.
interactions.artifactChange: Token-to-node overlays annotate the saved source.

evidence.observable: Opening and closing tags map to elements without becoming separate DOM nodes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source tags counted as independent tree children.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace one complete element from two tags to one node.

interactions.title: Predict DOM serialization
interactions.learnerAction: Predict which quote, case, spacing, and optional source forms may serialize differently while preserving the current DOM.
interactions.artifactChange: A DOM-derived serialization is generated for comparison.

evidence.observable: Prediction separates byte-level source form from node and property identity.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Byte equality assumed between source and DOM serialization.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare one authored token with its parsed property.

interactions.title: Add one annotated source example
interactions.learnerAction: Add a new volunteer topic element, then mark its tag ranges, element node, and text node without altering prior hierarchy.
interactions.artifactChange: A volunteer topic and source-to-DOM evidence join the page.

evidence.observable: Complete element, correct sibling relation, and text node all match annotations.
evidence.changedCase: Topic text changes while token-to-node mapping remains correct.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Annotation points to tags rather than the resulting element node.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate source ranges, parsed node, and contained text.

interactions.title: Compare source and live tree
interactions.learnerAction: Change indentation and attribute quote style in a disposable copy, then inspect stable nodes and changed serialization.
interactions.artifactChange: Nonsemantic source forms change in a comparison copy.

evidence.observable: Parsed elements, text, and relationships stay stable despite serialization differences.
evidence.changedCase: Tag-name case changes in another comparison and normalizes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Serialization difference mistaken for semantic tree change.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare node identity and properties before source spelling.

interactions.title: Explain source DOM boundaries
interactions.learnerAction: Explain how the orientation heading source becomes one element node with text and sibling relationships.
interactions.artifactChange: A source-to-DOM explanation is saved as evidence.

evidence.observable: Explanation distinguishes syntax tokens, parsed objects, content, and relationships.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Terminology without artifact-specific source ranges or nodes.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name one observed example for each technical term.

interactions.title: Choose a void element use
interactions.learnerAction: Choose the supplied content case that requires a void element and predict its following sibling behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; void applicability and placement are predicted.

evidence.observable: Choice uses element semantics and absence of child content, not short source length.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any empty-looking element assumed to be void.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare content models of one void and one normal element.

interactions.title: Add an orientation separator
interactions.learnerAction: Add the brief-required thematic separator using correct HTML void syntax at the topic boundary.
interactions.artifactChange: A thematic void element marks the approved topic shift.

evidence.observable: One void node occupies the intended sibling position without end tag or child content.
evidence.changedCase: A new paragraph is inserted after it and remains a sibling.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Invented end tag or following prose parsed as child content.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect the void node and its following sibling.

interactions.title: Challenge slash closure
interactions.learnerAction: Predict parsed output when a trailing slash is added to a normal paragraph start tag before following text.
interactions.artifactChange: A disposable normal-element slash case is parsed.

evidence.observable: Prediction rejects slash closure and tracks following text until a real end boundary.
evidence.changedCase: The slash is tested on another normal element.

interactions.feedbackTarget: XHTML self-closing behavior assumed in HTML.
interactions.correctionRoute: Locate the actual normal-element end tag and parsed content.

interactions.title: Inspect malformed void markup
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect a disposable invented end tag and explain how parser evidence differs from the author's intended closure.
interactions.artifactChange: A malformed void comparison is created and removed.

evidence.observable: Inspection identifies ignored or unexpected source token behavior and stable following nodes.
evidence.changedCase: A second void element receives a different invented end tag.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Closing-tag token assumed to create a meaningful DOM boundary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Map the closing source token to actual parser output.

interactions.title: Add a line-content break
interactions.learnerAction: Add one brief-required intrinsic line break and keep it distinct from paragraph boundaries and visual spacing.
interactions.artifactChange: One valid line-break void element joins address content.

evidence.observable: Break has no end tag, no children, and correct paragraph parent.
evidence.changedCase: Address text length changes while one intrinsic break remains.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repeated break tags or invented closing syntax.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restate the content line boundary and void content model.

interactions.title: Test void relationships
interactions.learnerAction: Run parser, exact cardinality, parent, and following-sibling checks on both orientation void elements.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit unless void evidence exposes a defect.

evidence.observable: Both void nodes have correct parentage and stable surrounding content.
evidence.changedCase: New siblings are inserted around each void element.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Void validation based only on source token presence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect parsed parent and sibling paths for the first failure.

interactions.title: Label start-tag attribute parts
interactions.learnerAction: Label whitespace, name, equals sign, quotes, value, and closing boundary in a supplied orientation attribute.
interactions.artifactChange: A start tag receives attribute-grammar annotations.

evidence.observable: Every part is selected inside the start-tag boundary in source order.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute placed after tag closure or inside end tag.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild the start tag one grammar part at a time.

interactions.title: Predict a missing separator
interactions.learnerAction: Predict tokens, parsed attributes, and errors when whitespace or the value separator is missing between two attributes.
interactions.artifactChange: Two malformed start tags are parsed in disposable copies.

evidence.observable: Prediction identifies exact grammar ambiguity and recovered parsed values.
evidence.changedCase: Quote style and attribute order change in a parallel case.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visible page output used instead of parsed attribute evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tokenize names and values before predicting behavior.

interactions.title: Add an element-specific attribute
interactions.learnerAction: Add the brief-required valid attribute inside the correct orientation element start tag with quoted value.
interactions.artifactChange: One orientation element gains a valid parsed attribute.

evidence.observable: Exact attribute name, parsed value, owning element, and behavior agree.
evidence.changedCase: The supplied valid value changes and reparses correctly.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute syntax valid but attached to the wrong element contract.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify owning element and attribute definition before editing.

interactions.title: Compare source and attribute property
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect authored quote form and the resulting DOM attribute name and value without assuming source serialization is preserved.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; source and DOM attribute evidence are paired.

evidence.observable: Authored syntax and parsed property are distinct but traceable.

interactions.feedbackTarget: DOM attribute value confused with surrounding source quotes.
interactions.correctionRoute: Point separately to source delimiter and parsed value.

interactions.title: Add two separated attributes
interactions.learnerAction: Add two brief-required attributes to one start tag using unambiguous whitespace, names, equals signs, and quoted values.
interactions.artifactChange: The orientation element gains two valid attributes.

evidence.observable: Both parsed names and values remain distinct and owned by one element.
evidence.changedCase: Attribute order reverses without changing parsed values.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Merged names, misplaced quotes, or attributes outside start tag.
interactions.correctionRoute: Parse each name-value pair independently before combining.

interactions.title: Predict duplicate-name recovery
interactions.learnerAction: Predict which parsed value remains when the same attribute name appears twice in one start tag.
interactions.artifactChange: A duplicate attribute is added in a disposable copy.

evidence.observable: Prediction identifies parse error and observed retained property without endorsing source.
evidence.changedCase: Duplicate values and order change in a parallel copy.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Both duplicate values assumed active or source treated as conforming.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect parser error and actual attribute map.

interactions.title: Repair duplicate attributes
interactions.learnerAction: Remove the duplicate while retaining the brief-required parsed value and all unrelated attributes.
interactions.artifactChange: The start tag contains one conforming attribute name.

evidence.observable: Parser error clears and intended value and behavior remain.
evidence.changedCase: Another attribute is inserted and uniqueness still holds.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Wrong value retained or unrelated attributes deleted.
interactions.correctionRoute: State the intended property before removing one source pair.

interactions.title: Find an attribute in an end tag
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect a malformed orientation fragment with attribute-like text in an end tag and locate the actual parser evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: A disposable malformed end tag is parsed.

evidence.observable: Inspection distinguishes invalid end-tag tokens from element properties.
evidence.changedCase: A different attribute-like token is tested in another end tag.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute assumed to describe the element because it is near its closure.
interactions.correctionRoute: Move reasoning to the element's start-tag attribute map.

interactions.title: Test attribute ownership
interactions.learnerAction: Verify exact owner element, attribute cardinality, parsed values, and changed-value behavior for the orientation attributes.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit unless ownership evidence fails.

evidence.observable: Attributes belong to intended elements and behave under valid value changes.
evidence.changedCase: One attribute moves to an invalid owner and is rejected.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Presence-only checks that ignore owner or parsed value.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect the first owner-property-value mismatch.

interactions.title: Explain attribute grammar
interactions.learnerAction: Explain how the orientation attribute source becomes a property on one element node and why malformed alternatives fail.
interactions.artifactChange: An attribute source-to-DOM explanation is recorded.

evidence.observable: Explanation connects start-tag grammar, parsed property, behavior, and recovery evidence.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Syntax recipe stated without owner, property, or behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace one attribute from characters through parsed value.

interactions.title: Predict boolean presence
interactions.learnerAction: Predict enabled state for absent, present, empty, repeated-name, and false-spelled boolean attribute cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Five boolean cases are evaluated in disposable copies.

evidence.observable: Prediction follows presence semantics and separately flags duplicate source.
evidence.changedCase: A parallel boolean attribute repeats all presence states.

interactions.feedbackTarget: The text false assumed to disable a present boolean attribute.
interactions.correctionRoute: Ignore value truthiness and inspect presence first.

interactions.title: Add a boolean orientation state
interactions.learnerAction: Add the brief-required boolean state using conforming presence syntax and verify its actual behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: One orientation control gains a valid boolean state.

evidence.observable: Presence, parsed property, and resulting state agree.
evidence.changedCase: Removing the attribute reverses state without a false string.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Boolean value spelled false while attribute remains present.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test presence and absence as separate source states.

interactions.title: Predict enumerated keywords
interactions.learnerAction: Predict behavior for valid, mixed-case, missing, empty, and invalid keywords of a supplied enumerated attribute.
interactions.artifactChange: Five enumerated cases are compared.

evidence.observable: Prediction uses the attribute's defined states and missing or invalid defaults.
evidence.changedCase: A different enumerated attribute uses a new keyword set.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any nonempty value assumed to create a custom state.
interactions.correctionRoute: List the contract's valid keywords and default behavior.

interactions.title: Add an enumerated behavior
interactions.learnerAction: Add the brief-required enumerated attribute with a valid keyword and verify parsed value and behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: An orientation element gains a valid enumerated state.

evidence.observable: Keyword, parsed value, and behavior satisfy the defined contract.
evidence.changedCase: A second valid keyword changes behavior predictably.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Invented keyword or correct keyword on wrong element.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify attribute definition, owner, and allowed keyword.

interactions.title: Inspect token-list values
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect how whitespace-separated tokens become a set-like parsed value without treating token order as universal meaning.
interactions.artifactChange: A token-list value gains and loses supplied tokens.

evidence.observable: Parsed token membership and element behavior update independently of source spacing.
evidence.changedCase: Token order and repeated whitespace change without membership drift.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Whole token list treated as undifferentiated free text.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect token membership rather than serialized spacing.

interactions.title: Contrast IDs URLs and text
interactions.learnerAction: Predict constraints and behavior for identifier, URL, and free-text values in three orientation requirements.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; three value contracts are classified.

evidence.observable: Each requirement maps to its specific value contract and owner.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All quoted values assumed interchangeable free text.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify how each consuming feature interprets the value.

interactions.title: Author changed value contracts
interactions.learnerAction: Add one identifier, one URL, and one free-text attribute from the brief with unambiguous source syntax.
interactions.artifactChange: Three orientation elements gain contract-appropriate values.

evidence.observable: Parsed values, owners, and observed behavior satisfy all three contracts.
evidence.changedCase: Identifier, URL, and text change independently and revalidate.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Correct syntax paired with wrong owner or value contract.
interactions.correctionRoute: Check owner and consumer behavior for each value.

interactions.title: Test invalid value recovery
interactions.learnerAction: Substitute invalid boolean, enumerated, token, identifier, and URL cases and record distinct observed outcomes.
interactions.artifactChange: Five controlled invalid values exercise contract behavior.

evidence.observable: Evidence distinguishes parser acceptance, conformance, defaulting, and runtime behavior.
evidence.changedCase: Parallel values use different invalid forms and owners.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All invalid values assumed to fail parsing identically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate syntax validity from attribute-value semantics.

interactions.title: Explain value-type behavior
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why the orientation boolean, enumerated, token, identifier, URL, and text values cannot share one truthiness rule.
interactions.artifactChange: A value-contract explanation is saved.

evidence.observable: Explanation names each contract and cites a changed behavior case.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Generic string explanation without element-specific contracts.
interactions.correctionRoute: Choose one pair with different missing or invalid behavior.

interactions.title: Predict comment visibility
interactions.learnerAction: Predict whether a volunteer author note appears in source, DOM inspection, visible output, and saved downloads.
interactions.artifactChange: A safe author note is planned but not yet added.

evidence.observable: Prediction keeps comments inspectable and rejects secrecy assumptions.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Invisible rendering confused with confidential storage.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect page source and comment nodes explicitly.

interactions.title: Add a bounded author comment
interactions.learnerAction: Add a non-sensitive volunteer-maintenance note at the relevant source boundary without commenting out required content.
interactions.artifactChange: A bounded safe comment joins the orientation source.

evidence.observable: Comment is inspectable, non-sensitive, correctly placed, and leaves required nodes intact.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Required content hidden or sensitive data placed in comment.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate author note purpose from user-facing content and secrets.

interactions.title: Inspect comment node placement
interactions.learnerAction: Locate the comment in source and DOM and explain why it does not create visible page structure.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; comment source and DOM positions are paired.

evidence.observable: Comment token and node are distinguished from element and text nodes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Comment treated as invisible element content or structural wrapper.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare node type and rendered participation.

interactions.title: Predict reserved-character parsing
interactions.learnerAction: Predict source, DOM text, and visible output for literal and referenced delimiter characters in volunteer labels.
interactions.artifactChange: Literal and referenced label cases are compared.

evidence.observable: Prediction preserves intended text without accidental markup or needless references.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every special character escaped or ambiguous delimiter left raw.
interactions.correctionRoute: Determine whether the literal is syntactically ambiguous in context.

interactions.title: Author unambiguous volunteer text
interactions.learnerAction: Use character references only where supplied volunteer text would otherwise conflict with HTML syntax.
interactions.artifactChange: Reserved volunteer text parses into the intended text nodes.

evidence.observable: Exact DOM text appears with no unintended elements or changed words.
evidence.changedCase: A second label uses a different ambiguous character context.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Unnecessary blanket escaping or accidental markup.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare intended text with tokenizer interpretation in context.

interactions.title: Explain comment and reference limits
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why comments are not secret storage and character references are text syntax rather than page structure.
interactions.artifactChange: A safety and syntax explanation is recorded.

evidence.observable: Explanation cites source inspection, node types, and rendered text evidence.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rules repeated without privacy or parsing evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Demonstrate source visibility and one text-node result.

interactions.title: Predict a missing close recovery
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the tree and attribute ownership when a normal orientation element loses its end tag before a sibling.
interactions.artifactChange: A disposable copy removes one end tag.

evidence.observable: Prediction separates authored intent, recovered tree, and content-model consequences.
evidence.changedCase: The missing boundary moves to another normal element.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Readable preview assumed to preserve intended sibling relation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Process boundaries and inspect actual parentage.

interactions.title: Inspect browser recovery evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Compare malformed source, parser diagnostics, recovered DOM, and visible output before proposing any repair.
interactions.artifactChange: No repair yet; four evidence surfaces are aligned.

evidence.observable: Observed recovered nodes are distinguished from authored tag tokens and appearance.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repair guessed from preview without causal parser evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Locate the first source-to-DOM divergence.

interactions.title: Repair the causal boundary
interactions.learnerAction: Make the smallest source change that restores intended orientation siblings and complete element anatomy.
interactions.artifactChange: The disposable missing boundary becomes conforming.

evidence.observable: Parser errors clear and intended sibling and parent paths pass.
evidence.changedCase: A new sibling is inserted and remains stable.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Broad rewrite or visual-only repair that hides root cause.
interactions.correctionRoute: Revert and change only the earliest divergent boundary.

interactions.title: Predict malformed attribute recovery
interactions.learnerAction: Predict attribute map and tree effects for missing quotes, merged names, duplicate names, and misplaced end-tag syntax.
interactions.artifactChange: Four malformed orientation tags are parsed separately.

evidence.observable: Prediction distinguishes tokenization errors, recovered properties, and later tree effects.
evidence.changedCase: Whitespace and source order vary across parallel defects.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All malformed attributes assumed ignored identically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tokenize each start tag before mapping properties.

interactions.title: Repair malformed attributes
interactions.learnerAction: Repair each malformed start tag minimally while preserving intended values and unrelated orientation content.
interactions.artifactChange: Four start tags become conforming with intended property maps.

evidence.observable: Parser errors clear and exact owner-value-behavior evidence passes.
evidence.changedCase: New valid values and an inserted unrelated attribute remain stable.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Correct-looking source with wrong parsed value or deleted property.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare intended property map before and after one minimal edit.

interactions.title: Test recovered tree stability
interactions.learnerAction: Insert new prose, list items, and attributes around repaired cases and rerun parser and relationship invariants.
interactions.artifactChange: Three changed insertions challenge repaired syntax.

evidence.observable: Valid insertions preserve the intended tree and property map without recovery dependence.
evidence.changedCase: A deliberately malformed insertion remains detectable.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repair overfit to original siblings or attribute order.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify the invariant broken by the first mutation.

interactions.title: Run parser-clean gate
interactions.learnerAction: Run parser errors, DOM relationships, void cardinality, attribute ownership, and text-node checks on the complete orientation page.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit unless the integrated gate exposes a defect.

evidence.observable: Current parsed artifact passes behavior evidence without source-token shortcuts.
evidence.changedCase: Volunteer content values and sibling counts change before rerun.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Aggregate pass accepted without inspecting failing evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Open the earliest parser or relationship mismatch.

interactions.title: Write one recovery diagnosis
interactions.learnerAction: Explain one defect as authored source, tokenizer or tree response, observed DOM, minimal repair, and stable changed case.
interactions.artifactChange: A causal recovery diagnosis joins workshop evidence.

evidence.observable: Diagnosis connects every causal stage and rejects preview-only proof.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Edit list without cause, parser evidence, or mutation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rewrite as source, recovery, observation, repair, verification.

interactions.title: Recheck prior correctness habits
interactions.learnerAction: Verify complete element boundaries, parent-child relationships, content models, headings, prose, and lists after all syntax additions.
interactions.artifactChange: Prior-module invariants are rerun on the evolved artifact.

evidence.observable: New syntax preserves all previously learned semantic relationships.
evidence.changedCase: A new list item and paragraph exercise prior invariants.

interactions.feedbackTarget: New syntax work allowed to regress earlier structure.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair the first prior invariant before continuing.

interactions.title: Mutate every syntax contract
interactions.learnerAction: Change one void neighbor, attribute value type, comment, character reference, and malformed source case while observing invariants.
interactions.artifactChange: Five controlled mutations challenge the completed orientation page.

evidence.observable: Valid changes pass their contracts and malformed source yields precise diagnostics.
evidence.changedCase: A second mutation set uses unrelated volunteer content.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Checks overfit to original serialization or values.
interactions.correctionRoute: State the contract invariant before reading each result.

interactions.title: Defend the repaired orientation page
interactions.learnerAction: Save, reload, and defend source-token mapping, void syntax, attributes, comments, references, recovery, and preserved prior structure.
interactions.artifactChange: The verified orientation page becomes the workshop completion checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Reloaded source, DOM, behavior, and defense agree across all concepts.
evidence.changedCase: One volunteer-data revision is saved and reverified.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Completion claim based on syntax presence rather than parsed behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Demonstrate the weakest contract with a new mutation.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A trail bulletin gains independent source-to-DOM annotations, void elements, attributes, safe notes, reserved text, and repaired parser cases as support fades.
activityDesigns.startingState: A saved trail heading, prose, and list are valid; a brief names new syntax behavior without supplying markup.
activityDesigns.completionState: The learner completes and mutates the bulletin with no copied orientation source and explains one correction from causal evidence.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback exposes observed tokens, parsed nodes or properties, and unmet contract while withholding replacement source.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Early hints name the evidence surface; later hints only retrieve a concept or offer a smaller unrelated syntax case.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires diagnosis, minimal repair, and a different trail-data mutation before the original checkpoint reopens.

interactions.title: Resume and retrieve trail structure
interactions.learnerAction: Resume the saved bulletin and verify complete elements, parsed tree relationships, and allowed list content before new syntax.
interactions.artifactChange: The prior trail artifact is reopened and checked.

evidence.observable: Reloaded structure matches saved source and prerequisite evidence.
evidence.changedCase: A new trail item preserves prior relationships.

interactions.feedbackTarget: New work begins on stale or structurally invalid source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore and verify the last stable structural revision.

interactions.title: Complete token-node mapping
interactions.learnerAction: Map supplied trail source ranges to element, text, and comment nodes without inventing closing-tag nodes.
interactions.artifactChange: A partial source-to-DOM map is completed.

evidence.observable: Every source range maps to the correct parsed object or no independent node.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Tags and DOM nodes counted one-for-one.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace one complete element through source and tree.

interactions.title: Test changed serialization
interactions.learnerAction: Change quote style, case, and formatting whitespace, then verify stable DOM nodes and changed source serialization.
interactions.artifactChange: Nonsemantic trail source forms change.

evidence.observable: Node identity and properties remain stable under valid source-form variation.
evidence.changedCase: Attribute order changes in a parallel fragment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Byte differences mistaken for DOM differences.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare parsed nodes before serialized characters.

interactions.title: Add trail void structures
interactions.learnerAction: Add one intrinsic line break and one thematic separator using valid void syntax and meaningful placement.
interactions.artifactChange: Two semantically distinct void elements join the bulletin.

evidence.observable: Both void nodes have correct parents, siblings, and no end tags or children.
evidence.changedCase: Longer prose and new siblings preserve their relationships.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Void syntax copied without semantic placement.
interactions.correctionRoute: State each void element's content role and parent.

interactions.title: Reject slash assumptions
interactions.learnerAction: Complete predictions for slash and no-slash cases on normal and void trail elements, then inspect the DOM.
interactions.artifactChange: Four disposable syntax cases are inspected.

evidence.observable: Predictions reject universal self-closing behavior and match parsed content.
evidence.changedCase: Another normal and void pair repeat the contrast.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Trailing slash treated as an HTML close operator.
interactions.correctionRoute: Locate real end boundaries and void content rules.

interactions.title: Complete attribute grammar
interactions.learnerAction: Finish two trail start tags by placing separated quoted attributes inside the correct boundaries.
interactions.artifactChange: Two elements gain valid element-specific attributes.

evidence.observable: Parsed owners, names, values, and behavior match the brief.
evidence.changedCase: Attribute order and valid values change independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute fragments outside start tags or merged names.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild each name-separator-value sequence.

interactions.title: Test malformed attribute variants
interactions.learnerAction: Test missing quotes, missing separators, duplicate names, and end-tag attributes against parser and property evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Four malformed trail variants are isolated.

evidence.observable: Each defect produces a distinct causal token and parsed-property diagnosis.
evidence.changedCase: Source order and values change in parallel defects.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All malformed cases summarized as syntax error without evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tokenize and inspect one attribute map at a time.

interactions.title: Explain start-tag ownership
interactions.learnerAction: Explain how one trail attribute becomes a property on its owner element and why an end-tag placement cannot do so.
interactions.artifactChange: A source-to-property explanation is recorded.

evidence.observable: Explanation connects source grammar, owner node, parsed value, and behavior.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute described as free-floating element metadata.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace from start-tag range to owner property map.

interactions.title: Complete boolean state cases
interactions.learnerAction: Complete absent, present, empty, and false-spelled boolean cases and predict the trail behavior of each.
interactions.artifactChange: Four boolean variants are evaluated.

evidence.observable: Predictions follow presence semantics rather than string truthiness.
evidence.changedCase: A different boolean attribute repeats the states.

interactions.feedbackTarget: False string assumed to disable presence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect only presence before value spelling.

interactions.title: Author mixed value contracts
interactions.learnerAction: Author one enumerated, token-list, identifier, URL, and free-text trail attribute from the brief.
interactions.artifactChange: Five contract-specific values join their owner elements.

evidence.observable: Parsed values and behavior satisfy every distinct contract.
evidence.changedCase: All five valid values change without source-shape dependence.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All values handled as arbitrary nonempty strings.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name the consuming contract before correcting a value.

interactions.title: Test invalid value outcomes
interactions.learnerAction: Apply invalid and missing values across the trail attributes and distinguish parsing, conformance, defaulting, and behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: Controlled invalid values exercise each contract.

evidence.observable: Results report distinct contract outcomes rather than generic failure.
evidence.changedCase: Different invalid values repeat the comparison.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Invalid syntax and invalid semantic value conflated.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate parser result from value-contract result.

interactions.title: Add safe trail notes and text
interactions.learnerAction: Add a bounded non-sensitive author comment and encode only syntactically ambiguous reserved trail text.
interactions.artifactChange: A safe comment and exact reserved text join the bulletin.

evidence.observable: Comment remains inspectable and intended text appears without accidental markup.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Sensitive data commented or every punctuation character escaped.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify privacy and syntax ambiguity separately.

interactions.title: Inspect comment and text nodes
interactions.learnerAction: Locate comment, element, and text nodes and compare them with source tokens and visible output.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; node-type evidence is recorded.

evidence.observable: Every source construct maps to the correct node and rendering participation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Comment or reference assumed to create structural elements.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect node type and visible text separately.

interactions.title: Complete recovery predictions
interactions.learnerAction: Complete intended and recovered trees for one missing boundary and one malformed attribute before repair.
interactions.artifactChange: Two recovery maps are completed without source edits.

evidence.observable: Predictions match parser nodes and properties and identify causal source tokens.
evidence.changedCase: Defects move to different elements and values.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Recovery inferred only from preview appearance.
interactions.correctionRoute: Find the earliest authored-to-parsed divergence.

interactions.title: Repair both causal defects
interactions.learnerAction: Make minimal repairs to the missing boundary and malformed attribute without changing unrelated trail content.
interactions.artifactChange: Both trail cases become conforming and stable.

evidence.observable: Parser, property, and relationship evidence pass after minimal edits.
evidence.changedCase: New siblings and valid values preserve the repairs.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Broad rewrite or symptom masking.
interactions.correctionRoute: Revert and repair the first divergent token only.

interactions.title: Run integrated syntax mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Mutate void neighbors, all value types, a comment, reserved text, and one malformed case while running evidence checks.
interactions.artifactChange: Five independent mutation classes challenge the bulletin.

evidence.observable: Valid mutations preserve contracts and malformed source remains precisely diagnosable.
evidence.changedCase: An unrelated trail dataset repeats every mutation class.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Checks tied to original values or source spelling.
interactions.correctionRoute: State the invariant before investigating the first failure.

interactions.title: Recheck cumulative structure
interactions.learnerAction: Verify complete elements, tree relationships, content models, headings, prose, and lists after syntax variation.
interactions.artifactChange: Prior-module invariants run against the completed bulletin.

evidence.observable: New syntax preserves all earlier structural and semantic correctness.
evidence.changedCase: A new topic and list item exercise prior invariants.

interactions.feedbackTarget: New syntax mastery claimed while prior structure regresses.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair the earliest prior invariant before saving.

interactions.title: Defend the trail bulletin
interactions.learnerAction: Save, reload, and explain source-node mapping, syntax decisions, one correction, one mutation, and preserved prior structure.
interactions.artifactChange: The trail bulletin becomes the faded-practice checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Reloaded source, DOM, behavior, and explanation support all concepts.
evidence.changedCase: One trail update is saved and independently reverified.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Completion described without correction or behavior evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Demonstrate the weakest claim with a parallel mutation.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A broken animal-adoption bulletin is diagnosed from token, property, tree, conformance, and changed-case evidence without wholesale rewrite.
activityDesigns.startingState: The saved bulletin contains slash myths, misplaced attributes, false-spelled booleans, unsafe comments, ambiguous text, and parser-repaired nesting.
activityDesigns.completionState: Every defect has a causal diagnosis, minimal repair, stable changed case, preserved adoption content, and saved evidence.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback exposes the earliest contradictory token, property, or tree observation and asks for a causal hypothesis before rule cues.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request hints point to evidence surface, then contract type, then a different mini-defect; corrected adoption source stays hidden.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A repair closes only after reproducing the defect and passing a different animal-data or sibling mutation.

interactions.title: Predict intended adoption structure
interactions.learnerAction: Derive complete elements, tree relationships, and allowed list content from the adoption brief before viewing malformed source.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; intended structure is recorded.

evidence.observable: Prediction gives a valid structure independent of current appearance.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Current broken preview accepted as intended structure.
interactions.correctionRoute: Map each adoption requirement to one valid node relation.

interactions.title: Locate source DOM divergence
interactions.learnerAction: Find the first source token whose intended effect differs from the parsed element, text, comment, or property evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; earliest divergence is marked.

evidence.observable: Diagnosis distinguishes token spelling from actual parsed object.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visible symptom selected instead of earliest divergence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace source and DOM in order until they first disagree.

interactions.title: Repair false self-closing syntax
interactions.learnerAction: Repair a normal adoption element incorrectly treated as self-closing while preserving following pet content.
interactions.artifactChange: The normal element receives a real end boundary.

evidence.observable: Following content regains intended sibling relation and parser evidence clears.
evidence.changedCase: A new following pet card remains outside the repaired element.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Slash retained as closure or content deleted.
interactions.correctionRoute: Locate the required normal-element end tag.

interactions.title: Repair misplaced attributes
interactions.learnerAction: Move attribute-like source from closing tags and after-tag text into the correct owner start tags with valid grammar.
interactions.artifactChange: Adoption attributes become parsed properties on intended owners.

evidence.observable: Owners, names, values, and behavior pass without altered content.
evidence.changedCase: Attribute order and valid values change independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Text deleted without creating intended properties.
interactions.correctionRoute: State owner-property pairs before moving source.

interactions.title: Diagnose false boolean values
interactions.learnerAction: Predict adoption states for present false-spelled booleans, absent attributes, and valid enumerated keywords.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; current value behavior is predicted.

evidence.observable: Prediction follows presence and defined keyword contracts.
evidence.changedCase: Another boolean and enumerated pair changes states.

interactions.feedbackTarget: String false interpreted as boolean false.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate presence semantics from keyword semantics.

interactions.title: Repair value-type misuse
interactions.learnerAction: Repair boolean, enumerated, token-list, identifier, and URL values using their distinct adoption-page contracts.
interactions.artifactChange: Five properties regain valid contract behavior.

evidence.observable: Parsed values and behavior pass under valid changed values.
evidence.changedCase: A second animal record changes every value type.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All values rewritten with one generic truthy-string rule.
interactions.correctionRoute: Diagnose one contract and owner at a time.

interactions.title: Expose unsafe comment data
interactions.learnerAction: Locate sensitive adoption data hidden in comments and explain every surface where it remains inspectable.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; privacy exposure is documented.

evidence.observable: Diagnosis cites source, DOM comment node, saved file, and download exposure.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Non-rendering comment treated as confidential.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect source and saved artifact outside visible preview.

interactions.title: Remove secrets preserve safe notes
interactions.learnerAction: Remove sensitive comment content, retain a bounded non-sensitive maintenance note, and repair ambiguous reserved text.
interactions.artifactChange: Comments become safe and required pet text parses exactly.

evidence.observable: No sensitive value remains while safe comment and exact DOM text persist.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All notes removed or sensitive value moved to another client location.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate safe maintenance purpose from secret storage.

interactions.title: Predict recovered adoption tree
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the parser-created tree for crossed boundaries, a duplicate attribute, and an invalid list parent before repair.
interactions.artifactChange: No edit; three recovered outcomes are mapped.

evidence.observable: Predictions match parser nodes, properties, and relationship repair.
evidence.changedCase: Defects move to different pet records.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Malformed source assumed to be ignored uniformly.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace tokenization and tree construction separately.

interactions.title: Repair parser root causes
interactions.learnerAction: Make minimal repairs to boundary, duplicate-name, and invalid-parent defects while preserving all adoption content.
interactions.artifactChange: Authored and parsed adoption structures agree.

evidence.observable: Parser, property, and relationship checks pass after bounded edits.
evidence.changedCase: New siblings, items, and values preserve repairs.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Broad rewrite hides causes or loses content.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair earliest divergent token for each defect.

interactions.title: Run adversarial adoption cases
interactions.learnerAction: Add a pet, change every value type, move a sibling, and insert a safe comment while rerunning all invariants.
interactions.artifactChange: Four mutations challenge every repaired concept.

evidence.observable: Valid mutations pass and deliberate malformed source remains diagnosable.
evidence.changedCase: A parallel animal dataset repeats the mutation set.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repairs tied to original values or source order.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name the first violated invariant before editing.

interactions.title: Write the adoption repair report
interactions.learnerAction: Save, reload, and explain each source cause, parsed effect, minimal repair, privacy decision, and changed-case result.
interactions.artifactChange: The repaired bulletin and causal report become the clinic checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Reloaded source, DOM, behavior, and report agree on every repair.
evidence.changedCase: One repair is reproduced in a different mini-case.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Edit summary without cause, evidence, or changed validation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rewrite weakest entry as cause, effect, repair, mutation.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An independent community-theatre callboard uses source syntax and attributes to publish stable audition information with parser-clean, mutation-resistant evidence.
activityDesigns.startingState: A blank saved file, theatre content brief, syntax behavior requirements, and server-canonical acceptance evidence are provided without starter markup.
activityDesigns.completionState: The learner submits a persisted callboard, source-to-DOM map, safe author notes, changed cases, parser evidence, and design defense.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Checks reveal observed nodes, attributes, behavior, and unmet competencies without revealing a tag sequence or canonical source.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Optional hints ask for the controlling source or value contract and point to inspectors; final syntax and placement stay learner-owned.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A failed requirement needs a learner diagnosis, bounded repair, and parallel theatre-data mutation before resubmission.

interactions.title: Plan valid callboard structure
interactions.learnerAction: Plan complete elements, parent-child relationships, allowed list content, and syntax-bearing elements from the theatre brief.
interactions.artifactChange: A learner-authored callboard tree plan is recorded.

evidence.observable: Plan covers required content with valid boundaries and relationships.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Syntax features planned before a valid semantic tree exists.
interactions.correctionRoute: Map each theatre requirement to one node and parent.

interactions.title: Author and map first content
interactions.learnerAction: Author the callboard title and audition prose, save them, and map source tags and text to parsed nodes.
interactions.artifactChange: The first saved callboard revision gains source-node evidence.

evidence.observable: Reload restores valid content and its token-to-node mapping.
evidence.changedCase: The production title changes and remaps without structural drift.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source tags treated as tree nodes or draft left unsaved.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace one complete element through edit, parse, save, reload.

interactions.title: Test serialization independence
interactions.learnerAction: Vary valid quote, case, attribute order, and whitespace source forms while comparing stable callboard nodes and properties.
interactions.artifactChange: Nonsemantic source forms change in controlled copies.

evidence.observable: DOM identity and behavior remain stable despite serialization differences.
evidence.changedCase: A second source-form set repeats the comparison.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Byte-level source form used as mastery evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare parsed node and property values first.

interactions.title: Defend the source DOM map
interactions.learnerAction: Defend one callboard mapping that includes opening and closing tags, one element node, text node, and sibling relation.
interactions.artifactChange: A source-to-DOM defense joins lab evidence.

evidence.observable: Defense points to exact source ranges and parsed objects.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Terminology without artifact-specific evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Select one concrete range or node for each term.

interactions.title: Author callboard void elements
interactions.learnerAction: Add brief-justified void elements for intrinsic line and thematic content without invented closing syntax.
interactions.artifactChange: Callboard gains two semantically justified void nodes.

evidence.observable: Each void node has correct parent, siblings, attributes, and no children.
evidence.changedCase: Longer content and new siblings preserve placement.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Void elements used for layout or authored with end tags.
interactions.correctionRoute: State content role and inspect following sibling behavior.

interactions.title: Challenge slash behavior
interactions.learnerAction: Test slash syntax on callboard normal and void elements and explain actual parser behavior without changing saved source.
interactions.artifactChange: Disposable slash cases are parsed and discarded.

evidence.observable: Evidence rejects universal self-closing assumptions and preserves normal boundaries.
evidence.changedCase: Another normal-void pair repeats the test.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Slash assumed to close normal HTML elements.
interactions.correctionRoute: Locate real end tags and void content models.

interactions.title: Author callboard attributes
interactions.learnerAction: Add separated, quoted, element-specific attributes inside the correct callboard start-tag boundaries.
interactions.artifactChange: Required callboard elements gain valid parsed properties.

evidence.observable: Owners, names, values, and resulting behavior match the brief.
evidence.changedCase: Attribute order and valid values change independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Correct-looking attribute text attached to wrong owner.
interactions.correctionRoute: List intended owner-property-value triples.

interactions.title: Test malformed attribute grammar
interactions.learnerAction: Create disposable missing-quote, merged-name, duplicate-name, and end-tag-attribute cases and diagnose parsed outcomes.
interactions.artifactChange: Four malformed cases challenge the learner's grammar model.

evidence.observable: Each diagnosis names source cause, parsed property map, and error evidence.
evidence.changedCase: Values and source order change in parallel cases.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Generic syntax-failure label without parsed evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tokenize one start tag and inspect its property map.

interactions.title: Implement distinct value contracts
interactions.learnerAction: Use boolean, enumerated, token-list, identifier, URL, and free-text attributes for separate callboard requirements.
interactions.artifactChange: Six contract-specific values become part of the callboard.

evidence.observable: Every parsed value and observed behavior satisfies its defined contract.
evidence.changedCase: All six values change through valid alternate states.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Generic truthy-string treatment across value contracts.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name owner and value interpretation for the first mismatch.

interactions.title: Test presence defaults and invalid values
interactions.learnerAction: Exercise absent and false-spelled booleans, valid and invalid keywords, token changes, and URL changes against behavior evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Value-state mutations exercise six contracts.

evidence.observable: Results distinguish presence, defaulting, membership, identity, navigation, and text behavior.
evidence.changedCase: A parallel theatre dataset repeats all value states.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All invalid values expected to parse or behave identically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate syntax, conformance, and behavior for one value.

interactions.title: Defend value contract choices
interactions.learnerAction: Defend each callboard value type using owner definition, valid states, observed behavior, and one invalid changed case.
interactions.artifactChange: A value-contract defense joins lab evidence.

evidence.observable: Defense is contract-specific and cites changed behavior rather than string shape.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute values defended as arbitrary labels.
interactions.correctionRoute: Contrast two contracts with different missing behavior.

interactions.title: Add safe notes and exact text
interactions.learnerAction: Add bounded non-sensitive author comments and character references only where theatre text is syntactically ambiguous.
interactions.artifactChange: Safe notes and exact reserved-character text join the source.

evidence.observable: Comments remain inspectable and non-sensitive while DOM text is exact.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Sensitive data hidden in comments or needless blanket escaping.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate privacy from syntax ambiguity decisions.

interactions.title: Inspect comments nodes and text
interactions.learnerAction: Map callboard comment and character-reference source to comment and text nodes and visible output.
interactions.artifactChange: Node-type and visibility evidence is recorded.

evidence.observable: Comments, text, elements, and rendering participation are correctly distinguished.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Comment or reference treated as structural element.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect node type and source token separately.

interactions.title: Repair independent parser cases
interactions.learnerAction: Diagnose and minimally repair one boundary, one attribute, and one invalid-parent defect in callboard copies.
interactions.artifactChange: Three parser-dependent cases become conforming.

evidence.observable: Causal tokens, recovered nodes, minimal repairs, and final invariants are recorded.
evidence.changedCase: Defects move to new theatre content and remain diagnosable.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Preview-only diagnosis or broad source replacement.
interactions.correctionRoute: Find earliest authored-to-parsed divergence.

interactions.title: Run parser stability mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Insert new siblings, values, list items, comments, and reserved text around repaired cases while running parser and DOM checks.
interactions.artifactChange: Five mutations challenge recovery independence.

evidence.observable: Valid changes preserve stable authored and parsed structure; defects remain detectable.
evidence.changedCase: Another mutation set uses unrelated theatre content.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repairs stable only under original source values.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify the invariant rather than serialized example.

interactions.title: Run cumulative correctness gate
interactions.learnerAction: Verify element boundaries, tree relationships, content models, headings, prose, and lists after all callboard syntax work.
interactions.artifactChange: Prior-module evidence runs on the completed callboard.

evidence.observable: New syntax preserves every earlier correctness requirement.
evidence.changedCase: A new audition topic and list item test prior invariants.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Syntax work passes while earlier semantics regress.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair earliest cumulative invariant before submission.

interactions.title: Save reload and mutate submission
interactions.learnerAction: Save, reload, change production data and every syntax contract, then verify current evidence is bound to the restored revision.
interactions.artifactChange: The callboard becomes a persisted mutation-tested candidate.

evidence.observable: Restored source, DOM, properties, behavior, and revision evidence agree.
evidence.changedCase: A second production update is saved and reverified.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Submission evidence tied to stale or unsaved source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Find earliest revision mismatch and rerun from there.

interactions.title: Submit and defend the callboard
interactions.learnerAction: Defend source-DOM mapping, void syntax, attribute grammar and values, notes, references, recovery, mutations, and prior structure.
interactions.artifactChange: The callboard and evidence package become the lab submission.

evidence.observable: Every concept is supported by artifact-specific behavior and changed evidence.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Completion claim based on syntax presence or step count.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reopen weakest competency claim with a new mutation.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An unfamiliar retrieve-first set reconnects prior tree knowledge with tags, void syntax, attributes, comments, references, and recovery without reopening examples.
activityDesigns.startingState: No completed artifact is visible; source fragments, token traces, DOM trees, and behavior observations appear independently.
activityDesigns.completionState: Every new and retrieved concept has committed retrieval, correction, parallel success, and adaptive delayed-review evidence.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback follows commitment and contrasts response with observed token, node, property, tree, or behavior evidence.
activityDesigns.hintContract: No pre-response hint is available; post-response support gives only a concept cue or smaller parallel item.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires causal explanation, a distinct parallel item, and delayed reappearance; rereading does not close evidence.

interactions.title: Retrieve prerequisite structure
interactions.learnerAction: Label complete elements, parent-child paths, and allowed content in an unfamiliar fragment before syntax analysis.
interactions.artifactChange: A committed prerequisite retrieval is recorded.

evidence.observable: Boundaries and relationships are correct without source-format cues.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Prior structure unavailable for syntax diagnosis.
interactions.correctionRoute: Complete a smaller parallel tree and reschedule retrieval.

interactions.title: Retrieve tags elements and nodes
interactions.learnerAction: Map opening tags, closing tags, element nodes, text nodes, and comment nodes in unfamiliar source.
interactions.artifactChange: A source-to-DOM map is committed.

evidence.observable: Every source range maps to the correct object or no independent node.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One DOM node invented per source tag.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace one normal element through a parallel fragment.

interactions.title: Predict serialization changes
interactions.learnerAction: Predict stable nodes and changed characters after valid case, quote, attribute-order, and whitespace variations.
interactions.artifactChange: Four source-form predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Prediction distinguishes byte source from parsed identity and properties.
evidence.changedCase: A second fragment uses different valid source forms.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source serialization mistaken for DOM identity.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare parsed properties before source spelling.

interactions.title: Retrieve void syntax behavior
interactions.learnerAction: Classify normal and void elements, predict slash behavior, and place following text in the parsed tree.
interactions.artifactChange: Void classifications and predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Responses use content models and reject universal slash closure.
evidence.changedCase: A different normal-void pair repeats the contrast.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Empty normal element classified as void by appearance.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare content capability and required end boundary.

interactions.title: Retrieve attribute grammar
interactions.learnerAction: Label and arrange start-tag boundary, names, separators, quotes, values, whitespace, and owner element.
interactions.artifactChange: A grammar arrangement is committed.

evidence.observable: Every attribute part is unambiguous and inside its owner start tag.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute outside start tag or merged name-value parts.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild one opening tag from labeled pieces.

interactions.title: Predict malformed attributes
interactions.learnerAction: Predict property maps and parser errors for missing quote, duplicate name, missing separator, and end-tag attribute cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Four recovery predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Predictions distinguish tokenization, property recovery, and conformance.
evidence.changedCase: Values and source order change in parallel defects.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All malformed attributes assumed ignored.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tokenize one start tag and inspect its property map.

interactions.title: Retrieve boolean and enumerated states
interactions.learnerAction: Predict absent, present false-spelled, valid keyword, invalid keyword, and missing-value behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: Five value-state responses are committed.

evidence.observable: Presence and keyword contracts determine behavior correctly.
evidence.changedCase: A parallel attribute pair uses different states.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Generic string truthiness applied to both contracts.
interactions.correctionRoute: State contract before reading serialized value.

interactions.title: Retrieve complex value contracts
interactions.learnerAction: Classify token-list, identifier, URL, and free-text values and predict one changed behavior for each.
interactions.artifactChange: Four contract classifications are committed.

evidence.observable: Each value is interpreted by its specific owner and consumer.
evidence.changedCase: All four values mutate in an unfamiliar fragment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Quoted values treated as interchangeable free text.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify how each consumer interprets its value.

interactions.title: Retrieve comment safety
interactions.learnerAction: Identify which author note is safe, which leaks sensitive data, and where both remain inspectable.
interactions.artifactChange: A safety judgment and evidence surfaces are committed.

evidence.observable: Response rejects comment secrecy and preserves bounded safe notes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Invisible rendering mistaken for confidentiality.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect source, DOM comment node, and saved file.

interactions.title: Predict character-reference text
interactions.learnerAction: Predict DOM text and visible output for literal, ambiguous, unnecessary, and correct reference cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Four text predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Responses preserve exact text without accidental markup or blanket escaping.

interactions.feedbackTarget: References treated as structure or required for every punctuation mark.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate ambiguity in the exact source context.

interactions.title: Predict parser recovery
interactions.learnerAction: Predict DOM and property outcomes for a missing boundary, invalid parent, duplicate attribute, and slash myth.
interactions.artifactChange: Four recovery maps are committed.

evidence.observable: Predictions identify causal source and actual recovered structure.
evidence.changedCase: Defects move to new elements and values.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Recovery inferred from visible output alone.
interactions.correctionRoute: Find earliest source-to-DOM divergence.

interactions.title: Correct a mixed syntax fragment
interactions.learnerAction: Minimally repair void, attribute, value, comment, character, and boundary defects while preserving content.
interactions.artifactChange: An unfamiliar fragment becomes conforming and behavior-correct.

evidence.observable: Parser, property, text, privacy, and behavior evidence pass after bounded repairs.
evidence.changedCase: New content and values preserve every repair.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Broad rewrite or token-presence correction.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair one causal contract at a time.

interactions.title: Correct cumulative regression
interactions.learnerAction: Repair a syntax change that regressed complete element boundaries, tree relationships, and allowed list content.
interactions.artifactChange: Prior structural invariants become valid again.

evidence.observable: Causal syntax repair restores all earlier relationships.
evidence.changedCase: A new sibling and item preserve the cumulative repair.

interactions.feedbackTarget: New syntax accepted while prior correctness remains broken.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair earliest cumulative invariant and retest.

interactions.title: Explain and schedule retrieval
interactions.learnerAction: Explain strongest and weakest syntax evidence, record corrected misconception, and schedule a different delayed retrieval item.
interactions.artifactChange: Review corrections and adaptive due concepts are persisted.

evidence.observable: Review record links misses, correction, parallel success, and delayed target.
evidence.changedCase: Later retrieval must succeed without reopening this set.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Exposure counted as corrected retention.
interactions.correctionRoute: Keep missed concept due until parallel retrieval succeeds.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A secure HTML-source bank samples token mapping, syntax prediction, attribute behavior, privacy judgment, causal repair, changed cases, and defense.
activityDesigns.startingState: No examples, hints, correctness feedback, or canonical source are visible during the scored attempt.
activityDesigns.completionState: Server-canonical item evidence records competencies, correction needs, and a different parallel reassessment path without issuing mastery automatically.
